title: Multi-Channel Setup
description: Configure OpenClaw to listen on multiple channels simultaneously, set per-channel options, start them all, and verify parallel message handling.

steps.instruction: Add Telegram, Discord, and a local CLI channel to the `channels` array in `openclaw.json5`.
steps.expectedOutput: `openclaw config show channels` lists three entries with types `telegram`, `discord`, and `cli`, all with `enabled: true`.

steps.instruction: Configure per-channel options such as allowed users, rate limits, and custom prefixes.
steps.action: Each channel object is updated: Telegram gets `allowedUsers`, Discord gets `rateLimitPerMinute: 30` and `commandPrefix: "!"`, CLI gets `historySize: 100`.
steps.expectedOutput: `openclaw config show channels --verbose` prints each channel with its unique options. Discord shows the `!` prefix and rate limit; Telegram shows allowed users.

steps.instruction: Start the Gateway with all channels enabled.
steps.action: The terminal runs `openclaw gateway start --all`. The Gateway initializes adapters for Telegram (long-polling), Discord (gateway websocket), and CLI (stdin REPL).
steps.expectedOutput: The log prints three lines: `Telegram adapter connected`, `Discord gateway connected (shard 0)`, `CLI adapter ready`. A summary shows `3 channels active`.

steps.instruction: Send messages from Telegram and Discord at the same time and verify both receive responses.
steps.action: Two simulated incoming messages arrive concurrently: one from Telegram user `@alice` and one from Discord user `bob#1234`. The Gateway dispatches both to the agent.
steps.expectedOutput: Both users receive replies within 2 seconds. The Gateway log shows interleaved dispatch entries confirming parallel processing: `Dispatching msg from @alice`, `Dispatching msg from bob#1234`, `Reply sent to @alice`, `Reply sent to bob#1234`.

completionMessage: Congratulations! OpenClaw is now listening on Telegram, Discord, and CLI simultaneously. Per-channel options are applied and parallel messages are handled without conflict.
